name: BOOK_EDITORIAL
description: Swarm editorial review and revision workflow. The most complex workflow in the BOOK family. Takes a storyboarded manuscript and audits it against declared templates (voice, persona, style, prose) for consistency, correctness, and quality. Uses a four-axis parallel junior audit → cross-axis synthesis → senior precision filter → senior final verdict pipeline. When issues are found, a REVISION worker surgically patches flagged passages. Templates (voice/persona/style/prose) are loaded from BOOK_MANIFEST.json and define what the editorial workers check against. The storyboard (STORYBOARD.md) serves as the structural reference — ensuring voice application doesn't break logical structure. Triggered by the phrase 'BOOK_EDITORIAL'. Owner: Michael. Orchestrator: QUEEN.

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN reads BOOK_MANIFEST.json from project root

QUEEN reads chapter_subset parameter (from invocation or from BOOK_MANIFEST.json workflow_settings; invocation takes precedence; if absent or null = full-manuscript pass); validates all slugs if subset specified (per CHAPTER_SUBSET_PROTOCOL.md §4)
QUEEN resolves templates from manifest (bundle or composition mode)
QUEEN validates template compatibility if composition mode (check against TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY matrix)
QUEEN reads STORYBOARD.md from project root
QUEEN verifies chapter files exist in chapters/ directory

QUEEN scans frontmatter of each chapter file in the working set for drafter_origin: true — notes which chapters are drafter-origin; passes this list to all junior workers and EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS as part of their context packets
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_EDITORIAL mode engaged. Templates loaded: [list]. [Subset: X chapters.] [Drafter-origin chapters: Y — enhanced scrutiny active.]'
QUEEN proceeds to spawn editorial workers

chapter_subset_parameter.format: null | [array of chapter slugs] | {from: slug, to: slug}
chapter_subset_parameter.source_precedence: invocation parameter overrides BOOK_MANIFEST.json workflow_settings; absent/null = full-manuscript pass
chapter_subset_parameter.behavior_when_present: all 4 JUNIOR workers and EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS receive only the subset chapter files for audit; STORYBOARD.md and STRUCTURE.md are available in full as reference documents; findings lists contain findings only from subset chapters; cross-axis findings are identified within the subset only
chapter_subset_parameter.note: subset restriction applies to chapter files being audited, not to reference documents (STORYBOARD.md, STRUCTURE.md, templates)

drafter_origin_pre_step.behavior: QUEEN reads frontmatter of each chapter file in the working set; chapters with drafter_origin: true are flagged in context packets for all junior workers; all four JUNIOR workers apply their full audit checklist to drafter-origin chapters (no reduced scope); JUNIOR_CONCEPT additionally checks every drafter-origin chapter for DRAFTER_GAP markers; EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS is aware that drafter-origin content carries higher risk for voice-style conflicts

template_resolution.spec_reference: templates/TEMPLATE_RESOLUTION.md — full resolution algorithm including all error paths and fallback behavior
template_resolution.compatibility_matrix: templates/TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY.md — compatibility matrix for all 13 pairs among the 6 initial atomics; validation algorithm in §6
template_resolution.bundle_mode: if manifest templates.mode == 'bundle': load the named bundle doc (templates/BUNDLE_<NAME>.md), which internally references constituent atomics via constituent_atomics: frontmatter field; load each constituent atomic as well
template_resolution.composition_mode: if manifest templates.mode == 'composition': load all four atomic templates (VOICE, PERSONA, STYLE, PROSE); then run compatibility validation algorithm (templates/TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY.md §6.2) across all 6 atomic pairs before spawning any workers

compatibility_validation.algorithm: templates/TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY.md §6.2 — full pseudocode with output format per case
compatibility_validation.on_requires_bundle_mediation: emit non-blocking WARNING with pair, rationale, and recommended bundle name (if any); proceed to spawn workers
compatibility_validation.on_not_tested: emit non-blocking WARNING with pair; proceed to spawn workers; note that EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS should monitor for cross-axis conflicts from this pair
compatibility_validation.on_conflicts: BLOCKING ERROR — emit ESCALATE with pair, rationale from compatibility notes registry, and resolution options; HALT all worker spawning; wait for human direction

template_resolution.on_incompatibility: if compatibility matrix returns 'conflicts' for any declared atomic pair: QUEEN reports the conflict, cites TEMPLATE_COMPATIBILITY.md §5 rationale, and waits for human resolution before proceeding. This is a blocking error. Non-blocking warnings (requires-bundle-mediation, not-tested) allow workflow to proceed.
template_resolution.on_missing_template: if any declared template file does not exist: attempt alias resolution (search all templates/*.md for aliases: field containing requested name); if alias found: load and warn to update manifest; if no alias: QUEEN reports missing file path and ESCALATEs immediately; no partial template loading
template_resolution.on_version_mismatch: if MAJOR version mismatches pinned version: ESCALATE; if MINOR/PATCH mismatch only: load available version and warn
template_resolution.resolution_success_report: after all templates loaded and compatibility validated without blocking errors: emit 'Template resolution complete. Mode: [bundle|composition]. Templates loaded: [list with versions]. Compatibility: [summary]. Proceeding to spawn JUNIOR_EDITORIAL unit.'

relationship_to_book_family.contract: BOOK_EDITORIAL produces polished chapter files (modified in place). BOOK_PRODUCTION validates and formats them for print.

required_files: all template files referenced by manifest (voice, persona, style, prose or bundle)
prerequisites.required_infrastructure: template files must exist in the templates directory; compatibility matrix must exist if composition mode

four_axes.VOICE: how the author relates to the reader — pedagogical/narrative contract
four_axes.PERSONA: who the author is performing as — domain authority, relationship to material
four_axes.STYLE: genre-level conventions — citation, argument structure, section patterns, density
four_axes.PROSE: sentence-level craft rules — complexity, register, metaphor, rhythm

template_document_structure.audit_checklist_usage: editorial workers use the Audit Checklist section as their primary evaluation framework

resolution_modes.bundle: single document specifying all 4 axes + synergy notes. BUNDLE_<n>.md
resolution_modes.composition: 4 separate atomic documents. VOICE_<n>.md + PERSONA_<n>.md + STYLE_<n>.md + PROSE_<n>.md

QUEEN.description: QUEEN is Claude in the main conversation when BOOK_EDITORIAL is active. Resolves templates, spawns all editorial workers, manages the junior→synthesis→senior pipeline, spawns REVISION on REVISE verdict, manages QA loop, executes verdicts.
responsibilities: template resolution from BOOK_MANIFEST.json (bundle or composition mode)
spawn EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS after junior unit completes
on REVISE: spawn REVISION worker, then re-enter full pipeline from JUNIOR_EDITORIAL
escalation on loop limits or blocking issues

JUNIOR_VOICE.stance: hypercritical, adversarial, high-recall — bias strongly toward flagging
JUNIOR_VOICE.description: Audits the manuscript against the declared VOICE template. Checks every chapter for adherence to the voice contract. Over-flagging is by design — SENIOR_SANITY filters false positives.
produces: each finding: chapter, section, paragraph reference + specific passage + violation description + which audit checklist item was violated
checks: pedagogical contract adherence (per voice template's Contract section)
narrator posture consistency throughout
pronoun usage patterns match voice template (we/I/passive)
uncertainty expression matches voice template
question frequency and placement match voice template
no unintentional code-switching into a different voice mid-chapter
all Characteristic Patterns are present where expected
no Anti-Patterns appear
JUNIOR_VOICE.drafter_origin_stance: for chapters with drafter_origin: true in frontmatter, apply the full voice audit checklist with enhanced recall bias; drafter-origin prose carries higher risk of voice inconsistency because DRAFTER was not applying authorial instinct, only template constraints; any voice deviation in drafter-origin chapters is flagged at one severity level higher than the normal threshold for the same deviation in author-owned chapters
JUNIOR_VOICE.cannot_see: other junior workers' findings — juniors are independent

JUNIOR_CONCEPT.description: Audits concept consistency across the entire manuscript. Not tied to any single template — content-level check that applies regardless of voice/style.
produces: each finding: specific concept + chapters involved + inconsistency description
checks: term consistency: same concept called the same thing throughout (no unnamed synonyms)
definition-before-use: every technical term introduced/defined before used as assumed knowledge
no contradictions: concept descriptions in chapter N don't contradict chapter M
concept completeness: concepts referenced in later chapters were established in earlier chapters (cross-ref STORYBOARD.md prerequisite chain)
notation consistency: mathematical notation, variable naming, unit conventions stable throughout
cross-reference validity: any explicit cross-references (see chapter N) are accurate

DRAFTER_GAP marker detection (drafter-origin chapters only): for every chapter with drafter_origin: true in frontmatter, scan the chapter body for [DRAFTER_GAP: reason] placeholder markers; any such marker is a blocking Critical finding unless it appears in [DRAFTER_GAP_ACK: reason] form (human acknowledged; downgrade to Low severity); gap markers indicate DRAFTER could not produce content from available source material and human authorial input is required before GREEN_LIGHT. Spec: workflows/BOOK/WORKER_DRAFTER.md §5.2 and workflows/BOOK/DRAFTER_AUTHORSHIP_STANCE.md §Safeguard 3
JUNIOR_CONCEPT.drafter_origin_stance: for drafter-origin chapters, apply full audit checklist with enhanced recall bias — drafter-origin prose was not authored by the book's author and carries no authorial intent; over-flagging is explicitly preferred over under-flagging for drafter-origin content

JUNIOR_STYLE.description: Audits the manuscript against the declared STYLE template. Checks genre-level conventions.
inputs: PROSE template (atomic or from bundle, for register/density checks)
produces: each finding: chapter, section reference + specific issue + which style convention was violated
checks: citation conventions match style template
argument structure matches expected patterns (per style template)
section/chapter structure follows genre norms
prose density within expected range (per prose template)
jargon handling matches style template (defined on first use, assumed known, glossaried)
mathematical/technical presentation follows template conventions
all Characteristic Patterns present where expected
no Anti-Patterns appear
JUNIOR_STYLE.drafter_origin_stance: for chapters with drafter_origin: true in frontmatter, apply the full style and prose audit checklist with enhanced recall bias; DRAFTER may produce prose that is internally consistent-looking but subtly misaligned with genre-level conventions (e.g., argument structure, citation pattern, section organization); flag all such deviations in drafter-origin chapters regardless of whether they would be flagged in author-owned chapters at the same degree

JUNIOR_FLOW.description: Audits logical progression, transitions, and arc adherence. Cross-references against STORYBOARD.md.
inputs: STORYBOARD.md (primary reference for expected arc and transitions)
produces: each finding: chapter boundary or section reference + specific issue + how it deviates from STORYBOARD.md
checks: chapter transitions: each chapter's opening connects to previous chapter's closing
argument arc: manuscript follows arc described in STORYBOARD.md
redundancy: no concepts explained multiple times without justification
pacing: no chapters disproportionately dense or sparse relative to their arc role
reader journey: at each chapter boundary, reader has what they need for next chapter
storyboard fidelity: what the chapter actually does matches what STORYBOARD.md says it does
JUNIOR_FLOW.drafter_origin_stance: for chapters with drafter_origin: true in frontmatter, apply the full flow audit checklist with enhanced recall bias; DRAFTER may produce prose that follows the storyboard's structural description at a surface level but fails to execute the logical transitions the storyboard requires; check actual chapter transitions and argument arc against STORYBOARD.md with higher scrutiny for drafter-origin chapters

EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS.description: Integration worker. Reads all 4 junior reports and finds cross-axis interactions that no individual junior could detect. The only worker that sees findings from all four axes simultaneously. Also passes through all junior findings (tagged with originating axis) as part of its integrated report.
produces: - all junior findings passed through (tagged with originating axis)
- NEW cross-axis findings with interaction annotations
cross_axis_interactions_to_detect: voice-style conflicts: voice is consistent but doesn't match genre style expectations
concept-flow conflicts: concepts individually consistent but introduced in wrong order for pedagogical flow
prose-voice conflicts: prose register shifts where voice doesn't call for it
style-flow conflicts: genre conventions followed locally but overall arc doesn't match genre expectations
voice-concept conflicts: voice asks questions about concepts not yet introduced, or voice declares when template says discover
compound issues: findings that appear minor on one axis but compound across axes into significant problems

drafter-origin specific: voice-style masking — DRAFTER may produce consistent-sounding voice that masks a concept gap; a chapter that sounds right in voice and style may still have concept_completeness failures that voice-consistency obscures; flag this cross-axis pattern explicitly for drafter-origin chapters. Spec: workflows/BOOK/DRAFTER_AUTHORSHIP_STANCE.md §Consequences for EDITORIAL Discipline

drafter-origin specific: compound drafter-origin severity upgrade — where EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS detects cross-axis compound issues in drafter-origin chapters, flag at Medium severity minimum (vs Low for author-owned chapters where the same interaction would be lower-priority); rationale: drafter-origin prose was not author-vetted before EDITORIAL and compound issues reflect real gaps in the drafting constraint set

SENIOR_SANITY.description: Reviews EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS integrated findings. For each finding: marks it 'real' (valid issue, must address) or 'overzealous' (false positive, drop) with rationale. Does NOT produce new findings — only rules on existing ones. Same role architecture as SDLC SENIOR_QA_SANITY.
produces: filtered findings: each finding marked real|overzealous with 1-line rationale
SENIOR_SANITY.does_not_produce: new findings — only rules on integrated findings from synthesis

SENIOR_FINAL.stance: independent final pass — may surface new findings, especially emergent or holistic issues
SENIOR_FINAL.description: Last word on the manuscript. Performs an independent pass over the full manuscript plus all prior reports. May surface new findings the entire pipeline missed. Emits the binding verdict. QUEEN does NOT override this verdict.
produces: if REVISE: consolidated actionable findings for REVISION worker (only 'real' findings, with specific passage references and correction guidance)
if ESCALATE: blocker description for human (e.g., voice break might be intentional authorial choice, concept inconsistency reflects genuine material ambiguity)
SENIOR_FINAL.authority: QUEEN trusts this verdict; does not override

REVISION.stance: constructive — prose writer under template constraints
REVISION.description: The highest-skill worker in the BOOK family. Receives filtered findings + full manuscript + all templates + STORYBOARD.md. Rewrites ONLY the flagged passages. Does not touch unflagged text. Must simultaneously satisfy template constraints, storyboard constraints, concept constraints, and local prose quality — all while making surgical edits that don't destabilize surrounding text.
inputs: SENIOR_FINAL consolidated actionable findings (passage references + correction guidance)
all templates (voice, persona, style, prose or bundle)
STORYBOARD.md (structural reference — don't break logic while fixing voice/style)
produces: revised chapter files (only files containing flagged passages are modified)
revision report: per-passage changelog listing what was changed, why, and which finding it addresses
constraints: SURGICAL: only flagged passages are modified — unflagged text is immutable
TEMPLATE-ADHERENT: all rewrites must conform to declared voice, persona, style, and prose templates
STORYBOARD-ADHERENT: rewrites must not break the logical structure described in STORYBOARD.md
CONCEPT-CONSISTENT: rewrites must not introduce new terminology or contradict existing definitions
CONTEXT-AWARE: rewrites must read naturally in context — surrounding unflagged text must still flow
MINIMAL: prefer the smallest edit that addresses the finding; don't rewrite more than necessary
triggered_by: QUEEN on REVISE verdict from SENIOR_FINAL

JUNIOR_EDITORIAL.description: All four junior workers run in parallel. They do not see each other's findings. Each audits one axis independently. All four must complete before EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS begins.
JUNIOR_EDITORIAL.independence_rule: juniors are independent — no cross-contamination of findings between axes at this stage
JUNIOR_EDITORIAL.subset_scoping: when chapter_subset is specified at engagement, each junior worker receives only the subset chapter files; juniors do not audit non-subset chapters; STORYBOARD.md and STRUCTURE.md are provided in full as reference documents regardless of subset
JUNIOR_EDITORIAL.drafter_origin_scoping: QUEEN's pre-step identifies drafter-origin chapters; all four juniors receive the list of drafter-origin chapters in their context packets and apply enhanced scrutiny to those chapters per each worker's drafter_origin_stance spec

EDITORIAL_PIPELINE.description: The full editorial pipeline: parallel junior audit → cross-axis synthesis → precision filter → final verdict. Every REVISE loop re-enters this entire pipeline from JUNIOR_EDITORIAL. No shortcuts.

actions: verify BOOK_MANIFEST.json exists with templates section filled
resolve templates (bundle or composition mode)
if composition mode: validate combination against compatibility matrix
if incompatibility detected: report conflict, wait for human resolution (blocking error)
if any template file missing: report, ESCALATE immediately
verify STORYBOARD.md exists
verify STRUCTURE.md exists
verify chapter files exist in chapters/
initialize INPROGRESS.md entry for BOOK_EDITORIAL engagement
report template resolution: which templates loaded, mode, any notes
QUEEN spawns JUNIOR_EDITORIAL unit: 4 workers in parallel (VOICE, CONCEPT, STYLE, FLOW)
QUEEN collects all 4 junior findings reports
QUEEN writes junior summary to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN spawns EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS with all 4 junior reports + full context
QUEEN writes synthesis report to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN spawns SENIOR_SANITY with integrated findings
QUEEN writes sanity filter results to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN spawns SENIOR_FINAL with filtered findings + full context
QUEEN writes final verdict to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN executes verdict

revision_cycle.performer: QUEEN spawns REVISION then re-enters editorial_pass
actions: QUEEN writes REVISE marker to INPROGRESS.md with SENIOR_FINAL findings
QUEEN spawns REVISION with: actionable findings + full manuscript + all templates + STORYBOARD.md
REVISION returns: revised chapter files + revision report
QUEEN writes revision report to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN re-enters editorial_pass from JUNIOR_EDITORIAL (full pipeline re-run)
qa_cycle_counter++

main_sequence: PRESTEP: verify prerequisites, resolve templates, validate compatibility
EDITORIAL_PASS: JUNIOR_EDITORIAL (4 parallel) → EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS → SENIOR_SANITY → SENIOR_FINAL
if REVISE: REVISION → full EDITORIAL_PASS re-run (loop)

actions: QUEEN writes completion summary to INPROGRESS.md
polished chapter files committed to project root
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_EDITORIAL complete. Polished manuscript ready for BOOK_PRODUCTION.'

REVISE.trigger: SENIOR_FINAL identifies fixable issues in voice, concept, style, flow, or cross-axis interactions

ESCALATE.trigger: qa_cycle_counter >= 3 OR SENIOR_FINAL determines issues require human judgment OR template resolution fails OR compatibility conflict unresolved
actions: QUEEN writes escalation summary to INPROGRESS.md (cycles taken, findings history, specific blockers)
QUEEN pauses all worker spawning
QUEEN reports to human: what's blocked, findings that may represent intentional authorial choices vs. genuine issues, template conflicts if any
QUEEN waits for human direction
ESCALATE.resumption: human resolves; QUEEN resumes from appropriate point (re-REVISION, re-editorial, or human edits chapters directly and re-triggers)
escalation_examples: voice break that might be intentional authorial choice (e.g., a chapter deliberately shifts voice for effect)
concept inconsistency reflecting genuine ambiguity in underlying material
style violation that the author may prefer over strict template adherence
persistent cross-axis conflict that REVISION cannot resolve without changing the storyboard

counter_semantics.qa_cycle_counter: increments on each REVISE → re-editorial cycle. At 3, auto-escalate.

isolation_rules.junior_isolation: JUNIOR workers do not see each other's findings. They are independent parallel auditors. Cross-axis interaction detection is EDITORIAL_SYNTHESIS's job, not theirs.
isolation_rules.senior_sees_all: SENIOR_SANITY and SENIOR_FINAL see the integrated report (all findings from all axes plus cross-axis interactions)

notes.complexity_note: This is the most complex workflow in the BOOK family. 8 distinct worker roles, 4 parallel first-stage workers, cross-axis synthesis, two senior review passes, and a constructive revision worker. The complexity is justified: editorial quality requires multi-dimensional auditing that cannot be reduced to a single-pass check.
notes.revision_skill_note: REVISION is the highest-skill worker in the entire BOOK family. It performs actual prose writing under simultaneous template, storyboard, concept, and local-context constraints. If the revision worker cannot satisfy all constraints simultaneously, it should flag the specific conflict in its report rather than silently compromising one constraint.
notes.template_authority_note: Templates are authoritative. Workers check against templates, not against their own aesthetic preferences. If a worker believes a template specification is wrong, the correct action is to flag it — not to ignore the template. Template evolution happens outside the workflow.
notes.storyboard_immutability_note: BOOK_EDITORIAL does not modify STORYBOARD.md. The storyboard is a fixed reference. If editorial review reveals that the storyboard itself is wrong (the logical structure needs changing), this is an ESCALATE condition that may require returning to BOOK_STORYBOARD.
